svg.title: The regime nobody can afford — and the two moves that change its cost class
svg.desc: One task is now routinely spread across 10+ context windows running 100+ turns. At a tuned SOTA serving stack — append-only prefix caching at its best — the shared working set still cannot be reused across the divergence and the eviction that the many-window, long-horizon regime forces, so it falls back toward re-paying the commons on every window every turn. fak makes the cache queryable and addressable: the shared work is computed once and stays reused across divergence and across bit-exact span removal, collapsing the multiplier. A second move, distinct: the permission check runs inside the prefill/cache/decode loop on the same call path, default-deny, with no outside seam to fail open — a property that is, at best, deeply buried in the rest of the industry.
svg.text: One task is now routinely 10+ context windows × 100+ turns. Even a tuned SOTA stack can't keep the shared work reused through that — for a reason that is structural, not a tuning gap.

g.text: WHAT CHANGED — THE WORKLOAD, NOT THE MODEL
Many windows per task is the default now. "10 agents" can be 10 file
analyses feeding one report, a planner fanning out workers, or a panel of
reviewers. Long horizon is the default too: 100+ turns, where agents
used to get lost after ~10. Both are now table stakes, not exotic.
WHY EVEN A TUNED STACK CAN'T AFFORD IT
Every window shares a big working set S — system prompt, tools,
shared docs, the report-so-far. Tuned prefix caching reuses S —
but only while the prefix stays whole. The regime breaks it,
so the commons drifts back to being paid A × T times.

svg.text: THE COST OF SERVING ONE TASK (the shared term is what bites)
cost ≈ A · T · f(S) + (each window's own new work)
A = windows on the task · T = turns each · S = shared working set · the disease is the A · T multiplier on the commons — and the win is removing it, not changing the model.

g.text: TUNED SOTA — APPEND-ONLY PREFIX CACHE (vLLM · SGLang · provider)
reuse holds only to the first split
Reuse is prefix-only and append-only: change context at position N and
everything after N is recomputed. The regime breaks that two ways:
• Windows diverge by design — each does different work, so the shared
prefix survives only up to the first divergence point.
• Forgetting breaks the prefix — evict a secret or a poisoned result and
the tail after it must be recomputed. Long horizons force eviction.
FAK — A QUERYABLE, ADDRESSABLE CACHE
the A · T multiplier → ≈ 1
fak owns the KV cache as a kernel object, so it is queryable — S survives
divergence + eviction: reach in, remove a span, leave the rest
bit-for-bit identical to a run that never saw it — witnessed vs
HuggingFace at max|Δ| = 0. (Shipped and tested.)
Measured head-to-head: 4.1× vs a tuned warm-cache stack
(50t × 5a, Qwen2.5-1.5B Q8, bit-identical tokens · 60.3× vs naive).
A SECOND MOVE, DISTINCT FROM THE CACHE — POLICY INSIDE THE LOOP
The permission check lives on the prefill / cache / decode call path itself
Most agent safety bolts a recognizer onto the outside of the loop — a pre-tool hook in another process, a sidecar over a socket, an LLM judge. The model can argue past a
recognizer, and when that outside thing times out the call usually runs anyway — fail-open, exactly under load or attack. fak puts the decision in one address space, on the same
code path as the tool call — default-deny, no IPC seam — the way a read() passes through the OS kernel before it touches a disk. You can't sweet-talk a gate that isn't a participant.
If anyone else offers this at all, it is deeply buried — the in-loop, same-address-space, default-deny reference monitor is not a feature the rest of the industry surfaces.

svg.text: The 4.1× is a committed measured head-to-head vs a tuned warm-cache stack (arm B), bit-identical tokens (commit 2bbda6f). The 60.3× is vs the naive loop, with arm A's prefill modeled + validated live ~0.4%. Don't quote 60.3× as vs-tuned.

The 4.1× is the favorable (homogeneous) batching regime — co-serving windows in one batch degrades when tool outputs are large + heterogeneous (open problem, §3b). Capability claims (queryable bit-exact removal; in-loop default-deny) hold regardless. "Collapses A·T" is the cost model, not a new attention exponent. All numbers → BENCHMARK-AUTHORITY.md.
